No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person.
The court adopts its tentative ruling. DOORDASH,INC.'s motion for judgment on the pleadings is taken off calendar for non-compliance with CCP 439, which requires the parties to conduct a "meet and confer in person or by telephone" before filing. The parties are ordered to meet and confer immediately.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Have Requests For Admissions Deemed Admitted
Off Calendar Jun-28-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. KELLEY J. GURAL, Esq. of Burger Meyer LLP (Tel: 949-427-1888) for defendant Doordash, Inc.;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT DOORDASH, INC.'s Motion To Have Requests For Admissions Deemed Admitted.

Defendant stipulates to the matter being decided by the pro tem judge. The pro tem judge and counsel for defendant (both appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT, DOORDASH INC'S (HEREAFTER EITHER "DEFENDANT" OR "DOORDASH") UNOPPOSED MOTION TO HAVE ITS REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION DEEMEDS ADMITTED AND FOR SANCTIONS IS GRANTED AS FOLLOWS:
(1) WITHIN TEN (10) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER, PLAINTIFF IS REQUIRED TO SERVE DOORDASH WITH FULL AND COMPLETE VERIFIED CODE COMPLIANT RESPONSES TO EACH REQUEST FOR ADMISSION. TO THE EXTENT THAT ANY SUCH RESPONSE IS OTHER THAN AN UNQUALIFIED ADMISSION, PLAINTIFF MUST SERVE AT THE SAME TIME WITHOUT OBJECTION A VERIFIED CODE COMPLAINT RESPONSE TO FORM INTERROGATORY NO. 17,1 AS TO THE UNADMITTED REQUEST.
(2) IF PLAINTIFF FAILS TO COMPLY WITH PARAGRAPH (1) ABOVE WITHIN THE TIME PERIOD SPECIFIED, EACH REQUEST FOR ADMISSION WILL BE DEEMED TO HAVE BEEN ADMITTED AND PLAINTIFF PRECLUDED FROM CHALLENGING EACH REQUESTED ADMSSION AND FROM INTRODUCING AT TRIAL ANY EVIDENCE CONTRARY TO THE REQUESTED ADMISSION.
(3) THE COURT WILL IMPOSE MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST PLAINTIFF IN THE AMOUNT OF ONE THOUSAND, SEVENTY-ONE DOLLARS ($1071.00) PAYABLE TO DEFENDANT AND ITS COUNSEL WITHIN TEN (10) CALENDAR DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER.

Counsel for the prevailing party to prepare and submit a proposed order to the pro tem judge at rennepar85@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Paul Renne; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Its Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Kelley J. Gural
Off Calendar Jun-28-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. KELLEY J. GURAL, Esq. of Burger Meyer LLP (Tel: 949-427-1888) for defendant Doordash, Inc.;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT DOORDASH, INC.'s Motion To Compel Responses To Its Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two.

Defendant stipulates to the matter being decided by the pro tem judge. The pro tem judge and counsel for defendant (both appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT DOORDASH, INC'S MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF TO SERVE RESPONSES TO DEFENDANT'S REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET TWO, AND SANCTIONS NO OPPOSITION HAVING BEEN FILED, THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS FOLLOWS:
(1) WITHIN FIFTEEN (15) CALENDAR DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER, PLAINTIFF IS ORDERED TO DELIVER TO DEFENDANT, WITHOUT OBJECTION EXCEPT FOR A CLAIM OF A/C PRIVILEGE, A VERIFIED RESPONSE TO EACH REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND THE RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS THEMSELVES./C
(2) TO THE EXTENT THAT PLAINTIFF IS WITHHOLDING ANY RESPONSIVE DOCUMENT ON THE GROUNDS OF A/C PRIVILEGE, HE WILL IDENTIFY THE DOCUMENT WITH SUFFICIENT PARTICULARITY TO ALLOW DEFENDANT TO CHALLENGE THE CLAIMED PRIVILEGE.
(3) THE COURT WILL IMPOSE SANCTIONS AGAINST PLAINTIFF IN THE AMOUNT OF ONE THOUSAND,SEVENTY-ONE DOLLARS AND FIFTY CENTS ($1,039.50) PAYABLE TO DEFENDANT AND ITS COUNSEL WITHIN TEN (10) CALENDAR DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER.

Counsel for the prevailing party to prepare and submit a proposed order to the pro tem judge at rennepar85@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Paul Renne; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)

HEARING RE: ATTORNEY SY NAZIF'S MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD AS TO PLAINTIFF KEVORK MARTIROSIAN.

The Court's tentative ruling instructed parties to appear.

Matter discussed.

The Court instructs attorney Mr. Nazif to contact the Court's chambers by telephone at 10:00 a.m. to conduct an in camera discussion.

In camera discussion held.

The Court rules as follows: Attorney Sy Nazif's motion is granted.

Mr. Nazif is to prepare and submit a proposed order using the mandatory Judicial Council form.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)
